subject: Computer Networking
title: SMTP — Sending Mail
description: A quick, practical guide to SMTP — Sending Mail.

hook.text: SMTP is the protocol that delivers email. It has been pushing messages since 1982.

buildup.text: Your client connects to the SMTP server on port 587. It authenticates, then sends the message.

discovery.text: SMTP uses simple text commands: HELO, MAIL FROM, RCPT TO, DATA. It's readable by humans.

twist.text: SMTP only sends mail. It has no concept of folders, read status, or inbox management.

climax.text: STARTTLS upgrades the plain connection to encrypted. Without it, your email flies in the clear.

punchline.text: SMTP delivers mail. That's all it does, and it does it well.

quiz.question: What port does SMTP typically use for email submission?
